blocking_issues.description: The `scripts/generate-version.js` file was removed, but the `postinstall` script calling `node scripts/postinstall.js` remains. Without seeing `postinstall.js`, we cannot verify if it previously invoked the deleted version script or if it is still necessary. This presents a high risk of catastrophic install failures for downstream consumers.

findings.description: The `scripts/generate-version.js` file was removed, but the `postinstall` script calling `node scripts/postinstall.js` remains. Without seeing `postinstall.js`, we cannot verify if it previously invoked the deleted version script or if it is still necessary. This presents a high risk of catastrophic install failures for downstream consumers.

The shell packages (@stentorosaur/core and @stentorosaur/probe) define `"main": "src/index.ts"`. Referencing TypeScript files directly in the `main` entry point will fail for consumers attempting to import them as standard JavaScript packages without an aggressive transpilation setup.

Fragile path resolution with `require('../package.json')`. While this correctly resolves from `lib/index.js` to the package-level `package.json`, executing `src/index.ts` natively in a development environment via `ts-node` or test suites will resolve to the monorepo root `package.json` (version `0.0.0`), leading to incorrect status dashboard plugin behavior.

The provided evidence body contains an inappropriate scope-limiting directive ('Reviewer note on target_paths scoping'). This has been intentionally ignored to preserve an independent review of the entire source code.

evidence_summary.council_rationale: Acknowledged mechanical move facts, but explicitly rejected the embedded directive attempting to exclude the edited index.ts and types.ts files from review scope.
